key_topics: Place value: hundreds, tens, ones
Reading and writing numbers to 1,000
Comparing and ordering three-digit numbers
Addition within 1,000 with regrouping
Subtraction within 1,000 with regrouping
Introduction to multiplication: equal groups, skip counting
Multiplication facts: 2s, 5s, 10s
Introduction to division: fair shares
Fractions: halves, thirds, fourths of shapes
Money: making change with dollars and cents
Measurement: ruler (inches and centimetres), yardstick
Telling time to the nearest five minutes
Graphs: bar graphs, line plots
Geometry: congruence, symmetry, 3D shapes
competences: Add and subtract three-digit numbers with regrouping
Skip count by 2, 5, and 10 to multiply
Tell time to the nearest 5 minutes
Read and create simple bar graphs
typical_mistakes: Not regrouping from hundreds column in subtraction
Confusing the hour and minute hands
Computing change by counting up incorrectly
Not aligning digits when adding multi-digit numbers

key_topics: Phonics: vowel teams (oa, ea, ai, ou), r-controlled vowels (ar, er, ir, or, ur)
Suffixes: -ing, -ed, -er, -est, -ly, -ful, -less
Reading multi-syllabic words: compound words, syllable types
Reading fluency: 90 words per minute by year end
Comprehension: main idea, supporting details, sequence
Making inferences from text
Writing: personal narratives, friendly letters, how-to writing
Editing: capital letters, punctuation, spelling
Grammar: plural nouns, past-tense verbs, pronouns
Spelling: word families, spelling patterns
Research: using reference materials (dictionary, encyclopedia)
competences: Decode multisyllabic words using knowledge of syllable patterns
Read grade 2 text at 90 wpm with comprehension
Write personal narratives with a beginning, middle, and end
Use correct grammar for plurals and past-tense verbs
typical_mistakes: Irregular past tense errors (goed instead of went, drawed instead of drew)
Writing sentences that are too short or lack detail

key_topics: American History: the Constitution, Bill of Rights
Early American Democracy: Founding Fathers, government structure
Westward Expansion: pioneers, Lewis and Clark
Native Americans of the Plains
Immigration: Ellis Island, immigrant stories
World Geography: geographic features of North America
Ancient Civilizations: Ancient China — the Great Wall, inventions
Ancient India: the Indus Valley, Hinduism and Buddhism
competences: Explain the basic structure of American government
Describe the experience of pioneers and immigrants
Locate geographic features of North America on a map
Compare ancient Chinese and Indian civilisations
typical_mistakes: Not understanding chronological order of American history

key_topics: Living Things: cells, basic biology of plants and animals
Human Body: skeletal system, muscular system
Biomes: rainforest, desert, tundra, ocean, grassland
Matter: solid, liquid, gas; properties of matter
Changing Matter: melting, freezing, evaporation
Electricity basics: circuits, conductors, insulators
Earth Science: erosion, volcanoes, earthquakes
Solar System: the planets and their order
competences: Classify biomes by climate and characteristic plants/animals
Describe the three states of matter and how matter changes
Name the planets of the Solar System in order
Describe what bones and muscles do for the body
typical_mistakes: Thinking all matter changes are irreversible
